Preconditions: Financial records exist for historical periods.
A historical report has been generated for prior periods.
Steps: Send GET request to /api/historical-reports/{reportId} with a valid historical report ID.
TestCases.Expected: The response should contain all prior business events without any missing or altered financially relevant records.

Preconditions: Financial record exists and is part of historical reports.
User is authorized to attempt deletion.
Steps: Send DELETE request to /api/financial-records/{recordId} for a financially relevant record included in historical reports.
TestCases.Expected: The API rejects the deletion with an error message indicating that deletion is forbidden to preserve historical integrity. The record remains intact.

Preconditions: A financially relevant record was modified but not deleted.
Historical report exists for the corresponding periods.
Steps: Modify a financially relevant record with valid data using PUT /api/financial-records/{recordId}.
Retrieve the historical report with GET /api/historical-reports/{reportId}.
TestCases.Expected: Historical report reflects the updated record without loss or distortion of prior business events.

Preconditions: Financial record exists.
User is not authorized to perform deletion.
Steps: Attempt to delete financial record with unauthorized user credentials via DELETE /api/financial-records/{recordId}.
TestCases.Expected: Deletion is rejected with an authorization error message and the record remains intact.

Preconditions: Financial record exists.
User authentication token has expired.
Steps: Send DELETE request with expired authentication token to /api/financial-records/{recordId}.
TestCases.Expected: Deletion fails with authentication error indicating expired token.

Steps: Send DELETE request to /api/financial-records/invalid-record-id-format.
TestCases.Expected: API returns validation error indicating invalid record ID format and rejection of deletion.

Preconditions: There is valid business content available in the system that has not yet been invoiced
Billing rules are correctly configured
Steps: Send a GET request to /api/billing/uninvoiced with valid authentication
Verify the response contains a list of uninvoiced business content according to billing rules
TestCases.Expected: Response returns HTTP status 200 with a list of valid uninvoiced business content matching billing rules

Preconditions: All business content has been invoiced according to billing rules
Steps: Send a GET request to /api/billing/uninvoiced with valid authentication
Verify the response returns an empty list
TestCases.Expected: Response returns HTTP status 200 with an empty list indicating no uninvoiced content

Preconditions: Use invalid or expired authentication token
Steps: Send a GET request to /api/billing/uninvoiced with invalid authentication token
TestCases.Expected: Response returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized with error.message indicating authentication failure

Preconditions: Authenticated user lacks required billing permissions
Steps: Send a GET request to /api/billing/uninvoiced with valid authentication but insufficient permissions
TestCases.Expected: Response returns HTTP 403 Forbidden with error.message indicating insufficient permissions

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid credentials
User has permissions to create invoices
Steps: Send POST request to /api/financial/invoices with valid invoice data
Retrieve audit log for the created invoice event
TestCases.Expected: Invoice creation succeeds and is recorded in the audit logs with correct action details, timestamp, and user ID

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid credentials
User has permissions to create invoices
Steps: Send POST request to /api/financial/invoices with invalid or missing invoice data
Attempt to retrieve audit log for the failed invoice creation attempt
TestCases.Expected: Invoice creation is rejected with validation errors; no audit entry is created for failed action

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid credentials
An existing invoice with ID invoiceId exists
User has permissions to void invoices
Steps: Send PUT request to /api/financial/invoices/{invoiceId}/void to void the invoice
Retrieve audit log entry for the void action
TestCases.Expected: Invoice is successfully voided and the void action is recorded in audit logs with details including invoice ID, user, and timestamp

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid credentials
User has permissions to create vouchers
Steps: Send POST request to /api/financial/vouchers with valid voucher data
Retrieve audit log for the voucher creation event
TestCases.Expected: Voucher creation succeeds and the action is logged in audit records with appropriate details

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid credentials
User has permissions to record receipts
Steps: Send POST request to /api/financial/receipts with valid receipt data
Retrieve audit log for the receipt recording event
TestCases.Expected: Receipt recording succeeds and is logged in audit records with user and timestamp details

Steps: Send GET request to /api/audit/logs with an expired or invalid authentication token
TestCases.Expected: Access is denied and response contains error message indicating invalid or expired token; audit logs are not returned

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid credentials
At least one invoice creation, voiding, receipt recording, and voucher creation event exists
Steps: Send GET request to /api/audit/logs?filter=financial to retrieve audit entries of financially significant actions
Verify that entries for invoice creation, voiding, receipt recording, and voucher creation are present
TestCases.Expected: Audit log response includes all financially significant actions with correct event data and user identification

Preconditions: User is authenticated but does NOT have permissions to void invoices
An existing invoice with ID invoiceId exists
Steps: Send PUT request to /api/financial/invoices/{invoiceId}/void
Check that no audit log entry is created for void attempt
TestCases.Expected: Void invoice attempt is rejected due to insufficient permissions; audit log does not record the unauthorized action

TestCases.TestName: Validation Success with Correct Request Data
Preconditions: System is running
User has valid authentication token
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with all required fields correctly formatted and valid
Check the response body for success indicator
TestCases.Expected: Response returns HTTP 200 with created=true and no error.message

TestCases.TestName: Validation Failure with Missing Required Field
Preconditions: System is running
User has valid authentication token
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource missing a required field such as 'name'
Check the response body for a clear error message specifying which field is missing and why
TestCases.Expected: Response returns HTTP 200 with created=false and error.message specifying 'name' field is required

TestCases.TestName: Validation Failure with Invalid Field Format
Preconditions: System is running
User has valid authentication token
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with a field 'email' set to an invalid email format
Check the response body for a clear and meaningful error message indicating invalid format
TestCases.Expected: Response returns HTTP 200 with created=false and error.message indicating 'email' has invalid format

TestCases.TestName: Validation Failure with Additional Unexpected Field
Preconditions: System is running
User has valid authentication token
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource including an unexpected field 'extraField'
Check the response body for clear error feedback indicating field is not allowed
TestCases.Expected: Response returns HTTP 200 with created=false and error.message indicating 'extraField' is not allowed

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token
Steps: Send POST request to /api/resource/create with invalid payload missing required fields
Observe the API response
TestCases.Expected: Response body contains an error object with a consistent structure and message indicating validation failure; created=false flag is returned

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token
Reference resource does not exist
Steps: Send PUT request to /api/resource/update with payload referencing a non-existent related resource
Observe the API response
TestCases.Expected: Response contains a consistent error message indicating missing reference; update success flag is false

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token
Resource is in an invalid state for the requested transition
Steps: Send POST request to /api/resource/transition with transition not allowed in current state
Observe the API response
TestCases.Expected: Response returns a consistent error structure indicating state violation with appropriate flags signaling operation failure

Steps: Send GET request to /api/resource/list with invalid authentication token
Observe the API response
TestCases.Expected: Response contains a consistent unauthorized access error message and error flag; no data is returned

Preconditions: User has an expired authentication token
Steps: Send DELETE request to /api/resource/delete with expired token
Observe the API response
TestCases.Expected: Response includes a consistent error message indicating expired token, with unauthorized access error flag

Steps: Send POST request to /api/resource/create without any authentication headers
Observe the API response
TestCases.Expected: Consistent unauthorized access error with explicit error message and no creation performed

Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token but lacks update permissions
Steps: Send PUT request to /api/resource/update with valid payload
Observe the API response
TestCases.Expected: Response returns a consistent error indicating unauthorized access due to permission issues; update flag is false

Preconditions: Record with downstream dependencies exists
Downstream processing for this record is completed
No reversal logic implemented for this record
Steps: Send a PUT request to /records/{recordId} attempting to change a critical field impacting downstream
Check the response for failure indication
TestCases.Expected: The record update is rejected with response indicating the change would invalidate completed downstream processing and reversal does not exist

Preconditions: Record with downstream dependencies exists
Downstream processing is completed
Reversal logic is implemented and enabled for this record
Steps: Send a PUT request to /records/{recordId} changing a field influencing downstream processing
Verify the response indicates successful update
TestCases.Expected: The record update is accepted and downstream processing is protected via reversal logic

Preconditions: Record exists with completed downstream processing
Field to be changed does not impact downstream validation
Steps: Send a PUT request to /records/{recordId} to update a non-impacting field
Verify the response indicates successful update
TestCases.Expected: The record update is accepted as it does not invalidate downstream processing

Preconditions: Record has completed downstream processing
Update request changes fields critical to downstream
Reversal logic flag or implementation is null or false
Steps: Send PUT request changing critical fields that invalidate downstream
Inspect response body for specific error message about missing reversal logic
TestCases.Expected: Response indicates created=false and contains an error message about missing reversal logic preventing update

TestCases.TestName: Verify Operational and Financial Records Linkage on Transaction Creation
Preconditions: Valid operational record data prepared
Valid financial record data prepared
API authentication completed
Steps: Send POST request to /transactions with both operational and financial record data
Verify response confirms transaction creation
Retrieve operational record linked to the transaction
Retrieve financial record linked to the transaction
Verify that the operational and financial records share a common transaction identifier
TestCases.Expected: Transaction is created; operational and financial records remain connected throughout lifecycle as evidenced by shared transaction ID

TestCases.TestName: Reject Transaction Creation When Financial Record is Missing
Preconditions: Valid operational record data prepared
Financial record data missing or null
API authentication completed
Steps: Send POST request to /transactions with operational record data but without financial record data
Observe response body for transaction creation status
TestCases.Expected: Transaction creation fails with created=false and error.message indicating financial record linkage is required

TestCases.TestName: Reject Transaction Creation When Operational Record is Missing
Preconditions: Operational record data missing or null
Valid financial record data prepared
API authentication completed
Steps: Send POST request to /transactions with financial record data but without operational record data
Observe response for transaction creation status
TestCases.Expected: Transaction creation fails with created=false and error.message indicating operational record linkage is required

TestCases.TestName: Verify Continuity of Record Linkage on Transaction Update
Preconditions: Existing transaction with linked operational and financial records
API authentication completed
Steps: Send PUT request to /transactions/{transactionId} to update operational or financial details
Retrieve updated operational and financial records
Verify persistent linkage between operational and financial records is maintained
TestCases.Expected: Updated transaction maintains operational-to-financial continuity with consistent linkage identifiers

TestCases.TestName: Verify Linkage is Broken if Operational Record is Deleted
Preconditions: Operational record linked to financial record exists
API authentication completed
Steps: Send DELETE request to /operational-records/{recordId}
Attempt to retrieve the financial record linked to the deleted operational record
Check linkage status between operational and financial records
TestCases.Expected: Operational record is deleted; system reports linkage broken or financial record marked as unlinked

TestCases.TestName: Reject Transaction Creation When Transaction Link Identifiers Mismatch
Preconditions: Operational and financial record data prepared with mismatched transaction IDs
API authentication completed
Steps: Send POST request to /transactions with operational and financial records containing different transaction identifiers
Observe the response for linkage validation
TestCases.Expected: Transaction creation fails with created=false and error.message indicating mismatch in operational and financial record linkage identifiers
